parameters.content:
# VIDEO SCRIPT ENDPOINT
## Standalone Video Script Generator

### Webhook for dashboard Video Scripts tool
- Receives topic, duration, style preferences
- Claude generates full production-ready script
- Returns structured JSON with timestamps, camera cues, B-roll

### POST Body Expected:
```json
{
  "topic": "Why most businesses fail at social media",
  "duration": "30s",
  "style": "energetic",
  "platform": "Instagram Reels",
  "content_pillars": "tips, behind the scenes"
}
```

parameters.name: messages[0].content
parameters.value: =You are an expert video scriptwriter. Create a 30 second script for: {{ $json.body.topic }}. Platform: {{     $json.body.platform }}. Style: {{ $json.body.style }}. Return JSON with title, sections, caption, thumbnail_idea.

parameters.content:
# KIE NANO BANANA PRO — IMAGE GENERATION
## High-Quality AI Image Generation

### API: api.kie.ai
### Model: nano-banana-pro

### POST Body:
```json
{
  "prompt": "Professional social media graphic...",
  "style": "photorealistic",
  "aspect_ratio": "1:1",
  "resolution": "2K"
}
```

### Polling: Check job status after 7 seconds
### Cost: ~$0.02 per image

# KIE VEO — VIDEO GENERATION
## AI Video Generation

### API: api.kie.ai/api/v1/veo/generate
### Model: veo3_fast
### Cost: ~$0.60 per video

### Flow:
1. Receive prompt and optional reference image
2. Create video task with KIE Veo API
3. Poll for completion (20s intervals)
4. Return video URL

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: false, error: 'Video generation still processing', retry: true, taskId: $json.data?.taskId }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ success: false, error: 'Image still processing', retry: true, taskId: $json.data?.taskId }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, phase: 3, message: 'Calendar events created', events_created: $items().length }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, phase: 2, themes_count: $items().length, data: $items().map(i => i.json) }) }}

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "max_tokens": 8192,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "You are an expert social media content strategist using the NETFLIX MODEL for content planning.

THE NETFLIX MODEL:
- Each month has 4 weekly 'shows' (seasons)
- Week 1: 'The Origin Story' — Personal stories, behind-the-scenes, relatable founder moments
- Week 2: 'The Proof' — Case studies, client wins, data-driven results, testimonials
- Week 3: 'The Method' — How-to guides, tutorials, step-by-step processes, tips
- Week 4: 'The Vision' — Industry trends, bold predictions, future-casting, cliffhangers for next month

CYCLICITY RULES (NO content type repeats on consecutive days):
- Monday: Hook Post (personal story or bold statement)
- Tuesday: Value Post (tips, education, industry insights)
- Wednesday: Social Proof (case study, testimonial, client win)
- Thursday: Carousel/Thread (multi-slide educational content)
- Friday: Video/Reel (short-form video script)
- Saturday: Engagement (poll, question, interactive)
- Sunday: Cliffhanger (teaser for next week)

GENERATE A 30-DAY THEME CALENDAR for:
- Month: {{ $json.body.month }}
- Products/Services: {{ $json.body.products }}
- Industry: {{ $json.body.industry }}
- ICP Summary: {{ $json.body.icp_summary }}
- Holidays/Events: {{ $json.body.holidays }}

For EACH of the 30 days return a JSON array of objects with:
- day_number (1-30)
- date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- day_of_week (Monday-Sunday)
- week_number (1-4)
- weekly_show_name (the Netflix show title for that week)
- theme (specific topic for this day)
- content_type (Hook/Value/Proof/Carousel/Video/Engagement/Cliffhanger)
- platform (primary: LinkedIn/Instagram/X/YouTube/Facebook)
- secondary_platform (for repurposing)
- hook (the opening line or attention-grabber)
- cta (call to action)
- hashtags (5 relevant hashtags)
- notes (production notes or tips)

Return ONLY valid JSON array, no markdown."
    }
  ]
}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, phase: 1, session_id: 'session_' + Date.now(), data: $json }) }}

parameters.content:
# ELUXR SOCIAL MEDIA AGENT
## With KIE Nano Banana Pro + Veo Integration (CORRECTED)

### API ENDPOINTS

**Phase 1:** `/webhook/eluxr-phase1-analyzer` (POST)
**Phase 2:** `/webhook/eluxr-phase2-themes` (POST)
**Phase 3:** `/webhook/eluxr-phase3-calendar` (POST)
**Phase 4:** `/webhook/eluxr-phase4-studio` (POST)
**Phase 5:** `/webhook/eluxr-phase5-submit` (POST)

**Tools:**
- `/webhook/eluxr-chat` (POST) — Chatbox
- `/webhook/eluxr-videoscript` (POST) — Video Scripts
- `/webhook/eluxr-imagegen` (POST) — KIE Nano Banana Pro
- `/webhook/eluxr-videogen` (POST) — KIE Veo
- `/webhook/eluxr-approval-list` (GET) — List Queue
- `/webhook/eluxr-approval-action` (POST) — Approve/Reject

### REQUIRED ENV VARIABLES
- `KIE_API_KEY` — api.kie.ai API key
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — Claude API key

### COSTS
- Images: ~$0.02/image (Nano Banana Pro)
- Videos: ~$0.60/video (Veo)

# 📌 CHATBOX API ENDPOINTS
## Dashboard Chat Integration

### How This Works:
Each phase's chatbox in the dashboard sends messages to a unified chat webhook. Claude processes the message with context from the current phase and returns a response.

### Setup:
- Single webhook handles all phase chats
- Phase number is passed in the request body
- Claude has system prompts specific to each phase
- Conversation history is maintained per session

### Dashboard Integration:
In your frontend JavaScript, the chatbox `sendChat()` function should POST to:
`POST /webhook/eluxr-chat`

Body:
```json
{
  "phase": 1,
  "message": "user message here",
  "session_id": "unique-session-id",
  "context": { any relevant data from current phase }
}
```

# 📌 PHASE 1: Business Analyzer
## ICP + Market Research

### How This Works:
1. **Webhook** receives business URL + industry from the dashboard
2. **Perplexity API** performs deep market research (competitors, audience, trends)
3. **Claude AI** analyzes the research and builds an Ideal Customer Profile
4. **Google Sheets** stores the ICP for use by later phases
5. **Webhook Response** sends results back to the dashboard

### Setup Steps:
- Configure the Webhook node — copy the Production URL into your dashboard's Phase 1 API call
- Add your Perplexity API key to the HTTP Request node headers
- Add your Anthropic API key to the Claude node
- Create a Google Sheet called `ELUXR_ICP_Data` with columns: `business_url`, `industry`, `icp_summary`, `market_analysis`, `competitors`, `content_opportunities`, `created_at`

### API Endpoints Used:
`POST https://api.perplexity.ai/chat/completions`
`POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`

# 📌 PHASE 2: Theme Engine
## 30-Day Netflix-Model Content Themes

### How This Works:
1. **Webhook** receives month, products, events, and ICP data
2. **Claude AI** generates 4 weekly "shows" using the Netflix model
3. Each week has a unique theme, content types, hooks, and cliffhangers
4. Built-in cyclicity — no content type repeats on consecutive days
5. **Google Sheets** stores the theme calendar

### Setup Steps:
- The dashboard sends ICP data from Phase 1 + user inputs (month, products, holidays)
- Claude uses a specialized prompt for the Netflix content model
- Output: 30-day theme calendar with daily content types, themes, and hooks
- Create sheet `ELUXR_Themes` with columns: `month`, `week_number`, `day`, `date`, `theme`, `content_type`, `platform`, `hook`, `notes`

### Netflix Model Rules:
- Week 1: "The Origin Story" — Personal, relatable
- Week 2: "The Proof" — Case studies, data, results
- Week 3: "The Method" — How-tos, tutorials, value
- Week 4: "The Vision" — Future trends, cliffhangers
- Each day has a different content format
- Sunday always has a "next week teaser"

# 📌 PHASE 3: Content Calendar
## Google Calendar Sync

### How This Works:
1. **Webhook** receives the theme calendar from Phase 2 (or triggers from stored themes)
2. **Google Sheets** reads the stored theme data for the target month
3. **Loop** iterates over each day's theme
4. **Google Calendar** creates an event for each content slot with full details
5. Each event includes: theme, content type, platform, hook, CTA, hashtags

### Setup Steps:
- Connect Google Calendar OAuth credentials
- Select the target calendar (create one called `ELUXR Content Calendar`)
- Events are created with the content details in the description
- Color-coding by platform: LinkedIn=Blue, Instagram=Pink, X=Cyan, YouTube=Red, Facebook=Navy

### Event Format:
- **Title**: `[Platform] Content Type — Theme`
- **Description**: Full content brief with hook, CTA, hashtags
- **Time**: Uses optimal posting times per platform
- **Reminders**: 2 hours before + 30 min before

# 📌 PHASE 4: Content Studio
## AI Writing + Image Gen + Video Scripts

### How This Works:
1. **CRON Trigger** fires daily at 6 AM (or Webhook from dashboard)
2. **Google Sheets** reads today's theme/content brief
3. **Router** branches into 3 parallel tracks:
   - **Track A — Text Content**: Claude writes platform-specific posts
   - **Track B — Image Generation**: Nano Banna Pro creates visuals
   - **Track C — Video Scripts**: Claude generates camera-ready scripts
4. All outputs merge and go to the Approval Queue (Phase 5)

### Setup Steps:
- CRON: Set to run daily at 06:00 in your timezone
- Claude prompts are customized per platform (LinkedIn long-form, Instagram caption+hashtags, X threads, etc.)
- Nano Banna Pro: Add your API key. Supports styles: photorealistic, illustration, 3d-render, cinematic, minimalist
- Video scripts include: hook, setup, value delivery, CTA, B-roll suggestions, on-screen text cues

### API Endpoints:
`POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages` — Content writing
`POST https://api.nanobanna.pro/v1/generate` — Image generation
`POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages` — Video scripts

# 📌 PHASE 5: Approval Queue (Web App)
## No Telegram — Dashboard Only

### How This Works:
1. Content from Phase 4 gets saved to Google Sheets with status 'pending'
2. Dashboard calls `/webhook/eluxr-approval-list` to fetch all pending items
3. User clicks Approve/Reject in the web app
4. Dashboard calls `/webhook/eluxr-approval-action` with the action
5. Google Sheet row gets updated with new status

### Endpoints:
- `GET /webhook/eluxr-approval-list` — Returns all items from the queue
- `POST /webhook/eluxr-approval-action` — Approve/reject an item
- `POST /webhook/eluxr-phase5-submit` — Add new content to the queue

### Google Sheet: `ELUXR_Approval_Queue`
Columns: id | title | content | content_type | platform | status | scheduled_for | created_at | reviewed_at | feedback

### Status Values:
- `pending` — Awaiting review
- `approved` — Ready to publish
- `rejected` — Needs revision
- `published` — Already posted

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, message: 'Pending content cleared', deleted_count: $items().length }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, action: $('Approval Action Webhook').item.json.body.action, date: $('Approval Action Webhook').item.json.body.date }) }}

parameters.content:
# 📌 THEMES LIST ENDPOINT
## Read Weekly Themes from Google Sheets

### GET /webhook/eluxr-themes-list

Returns all themes from ELUXR_Themes sheet.
Used by dashboard to load weekly content schedule.

### Response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "count": 30,
  "themes": [
    {
      "date": "2025-01-01",
      "week_number": 1,
      "weekly_show_name": "The Origin Story",
      "theme": "Company founding story",
      "content_type": "Hook Post",
      "platform": "LinkedIn",
      "hook": "The day everything changed..."
    }
  ]
}
```
